originalTitle: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
summary: The companies were found guilty by a French court over an air disaster which killed 228 people.
content:
Air France and Airbus have been found guilty of manslaughter over a 2009 plane crash which killed 228 people.

The Paris Appeals Court found the airline and aircraft manufacturer guilty of corporate manslaughter over the incident, in which flight AF447 between Rio de Janeiro and Paris crashed into the Atlantic Ocean.

The passenger jet stalled during a storm and plunged into the water, killing all on board.

A court had previously cleared the companies in April 2023 but they were found guilty after this appeal.

The Airbus A330 vanished from radars during a storm on 1 June 2009, with its wreckage found after a long search of 10,000 sq km (3,860 sq miles) of sea floor.

The black box was found after months of deep-sea searches in 2011.

All 12 crew members and 216 passengers on board the flight were killed when the plane crashed into the sea from a height of 38,000ft (11,580m), making it the deadliest incident in French aviation history.

Relatives of some of the passengers, which included mainly French, Brazilian and German nationals, gathered to hear the verdict on Thursday.

The companies have been asked to pay the maximum fine, €225,000 ($261,720; £194,500) each - but some of the victims' families have criticised the amount as a token penalty.

However, the ruling may be seen as causing damage to the companies' reputations.

During their closing arguments in November, the deputy prosecutors said the companies' behaviour had been "unacceptable", accusing them of "spouting nonsense and pulling arguments out of thin air".

Both Airbus and Air France had repeatedly denied the charges, and French lawyers believe they are likely to launch further appeals.

The BBC has contacted Airbus and Air France for comment.

The crash led to a complex recovery operation in a remote part of the Atlantic Ocean, more than 700 miles (1127km) from the coast of South America.

During the initial searches, the French government had been responsible for investigating the crash and Brazilian forces took charge of retrieving the bodies.

In the first 26 days of searches, 51 bodies were recovered, many still buckled into their seats.

One family member who spoke to BBC News Brasil in 2019 said he had only been able to bury his son's remains over two years after the incident.

His son, 40-year-old engineer Nelson Marinho Filho, nearly missed the flight out of Rio de Janeiro's Galeão International Airport and was the last person to board, according to Air France staff.
chineseTitle: 法航和空客因2009年飞机失事被判过失杀人罪
chineseSummary: 法国一家法院因造成 228 人死亡的空难而判定这两家公司有罪。
chineseContent:
法航和空中客车公司因 2009 年导致 228 人死亡的飞机失事被判犯有过失杀人罪。

巴黎上诉法院裁定这家航空公司和飞机制造商在里约热内卢和巴黎之间的 AF447 航班坠入大西洋的事件中犯有企业过失杀人罪。

这架客机在暴风雨中失速并坠入水中，机上人员全部遇难。

法院此前于 2023 年 4 月裁定这些公司无罪，但在此次上诉后被判有罪。

2009 年 6 月 1 日的一场暴风雨中，空客 A330 从雷达上消失，经过对 10,000 平方公里（3,860 平方英里）海底的长时间搜寻后，发现了其残骸。

2011年，经过数月的深海搜寻，黑匣子被发现。

飞机从38,000英尺（11,580m）高空坠入大海，机上12名机组人员和216名乘客全部遇难，成为法国航空史上最致命的事件。

一些乘客的亲属（主要包括法国人、巴西人和德国人）周四聚集在一起聆听判决。

这些公司被要求支付最高罚款，每家 225,000 欧元（261,720 美元；194,500 英镑），但一些受害者家属批评这一金额只是象征性的罚款。

然而，该裁决可能被视为对公司声誉造成损害。

在11月的结案陈词中，副检察官表示，这些公司的行为“不可接受”，指责它们“胡言乱语，凭空捏造论点”。

空客和法航都一再否认这些指控，法国律师相信他们可能会发起进一步上诉。

BBC 已联系空中客车公司和法国航空征求意见。

坠机事件导致在距南美洲海岸 700 多英里（1127 公里）的大西洋偏远地区进行了复杂的救援行动。

在最初的搜查过程中，法国政府负责调查坠机事件，巴西军队负责打捞尸体。

在最初 26 天的搜索中，共找到了 51 具尸体，其中许多尸体仍扣在座位上。

一名家庭成员在 2019 年接受 BBC 巴西新闻采访时表示，事件发生两年多后，他才得以埋葬儿子的遗体。

据法航工作人员称，他的儿子、40 岁的工程师 Nelson Marinho Filho 差点错过了从里约热内卢加利昂国际机场起飞的航班，并且是最后一个登机的人。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
